content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 31 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 116v–116r (LC PDF pp. 229–230) | **Source Images:** DEU_031_page_001.jpg, DEU_031_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 162–163 (pa_0162–pa_0163)

---

**1** And Moshe went and spoke these words to all Yisra'el. **2** And he said to them: "I am one hundred and twenty years old today; I am no longer able to go out and to come in; and the LORD has said to me: 'You shall not cross over this Yarden.' **3** The LORD your God — he is the one crossing over before you; he will destroy these nations from before you, and you shall dispossess them. Yehoshua — he is the one crossing over before you, as the LORD has spoken. **4** And the LORD will do to them as he did to Sichon and to Og, the kings of the Amorites, and to their land, when he destroyed them. **5** And the LORD will give them before you, and you shall do to them according to all the commandment which I have commanded you. **6** Be strong and courageous [chizqu ve-imtsu]; do not be afraid and do not be terrified before them, for the LORD your God — he is the one walking with you; he will not leave you and he will not forsake you."

**7** And Moshe called to Yehoshua and said to him before the eyes of all Yisra'el: "Be strong and courageous [chazaq ve-emats], for you are the one who will go with this people into the land which the LORD swore to their fathers to give to them, and you shall cause them to inherit it. **8** And the LORD — he is the one walking before you; he will be with you; he will not leave you and he will not forsake you. Do not be afraid and do not be dismayed."

**9** And Moshe wrote this instruction [torah] and gave it to the priests, the sons of Levi, who were carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD, and to all the elders of Yisra'el. **10** And Moshe commanded them, saying: "At the end of seven years, at the appointed time of the year of release, at the Feast of Booths, **11** when all Yisra'el comes to appear before the LORD your God in the place which he will choose, you shall read this instruction [torah] before all Yisra'el, in their ears. **12** Assemble the people — the men and the women and the little ones, and your sojourner who is within your gates — so that they may hear and so that they may learn, and they shall fear the LORD your God and they shall take care to do all the words of this instruction [torah]. **13** And their children, who have not known, shall hear and shall learn to fear the LORD your God all the days that you are living upon the land which you are crossing the Yarden there to possess it."

**14** And the LORD said to Moshe: "Behold, your days are drawing near to die. Call Yehoshua and station yourselves in the tent of meeting, and I will commission him." And Moshe and Yehoshua went and stationed themselves in the tent of meeting. **15** And the LORD appeared in the tent in a pillar of cloud, and the pillar of cloud stood over the entrance of the tent.

**16** And the LORD said to Moshe: "Behold, you are about to lie down with your fathers, and this people will rise up and go whoring after the foreign gods of the land into which they are going into its midst, and they will forsake me and break my covenant which I cut with them. **17** And my anger will be kindled against them on that day, and I will forsake them, and I will hide my face from them, and they will be devoured, and many evils and troubles will find them, and they will say on that day: 'Is it not because our God is not in our midst that these evils have found us?' **18** And I will surely hide my face on that day because of all the evil which they have done, for they have turned to other gods. **19** And now, write for yourselves this song and teach it to the sons of Yisra'el; put it in their mouths, so that this song may be for me as a witness against the sons of Yisra'el. **20** For I will bring them into the land which I swore to their fathers, flowing with milk and honey, and they will eat and be satisfied and grow fat, and they will turn to other gods and serve them and despise me and break my covenant. **21** And it shall be, when many evils and troubles find them, that this song shall answer before them as a witness — for it will not be forgotten from the mouth of their offspring — for I know their inclination [yitser], which they are doing today, before I bring them into the land which I swore."

**22** And Moshe wrote this song on that day and taught it to the sons of Yisra'el.

**23** And he commissioned Yehoshua son of Nun and said: "Be strong and courageous [chazaq ve-emats], for you will bring the sons of Yisra'el into the land which I swore to them, and I will be with you."

**24** And it came about, when Moshe finished writing the words of this instruction [torah] in a scroll, unto their completion, **25** that Moshe commanded the Levites who were carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD, saying: **26** "Take this scroll of the instruction [sefer ha-torah] and place it at the side of the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God, and it shall be there as a witness against you. **27** For I know your rebellion and your stiff neck. Behold, while I am still alive with you today, you have been rebellious against the LORD; and how much more after my death! **28** Assemble to me all the elders of your tribes and your officials, and I will speak in their ears these words and I will call heaven and earth to witness against them. **29** For I know that after my death you will surely act corruptly and you will turn from the way which I commanded you; and evil will befall you in the latter days, for you will do evil in the eyes of the LORD to provoke him to anger through the work of your hands."

**30** And Moshe spoke in the ears of all the assembly of Yisra'el the words of this song, until their completion:

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**V.1 — "went":** The LC reads וַיֵּלֶךְ (vayelekh); the LXX Vaticanus renders this as καὶ συνετέλεσεν Μωυσῆς λαλῶν ("and Moshe finished speaking"), reflecting a Hebraic Vorlage reading וַיְכַל ("and he finished"). This is a significant textual variant: the Hebrew tradition presents Moshe physically going out to address the people; the Greek tradition resolves an implied awkwardness. The LC reading is preserved here.

**V.6 — chizqu ve-imtsu:** The dual imperative (Qal plural) is rendered literally "be strong and courageous." The LXX ἀνδρίζου καὶ ἴσχυε uses singular imperatives, indicating the Greek addresses Yehoshua alone here (conflating vv.6 and 7). The Hebrew addresses all Yisra'el in v.6, then Yehoshua individually in v.7 — a distinction EMET preserves.

**V.9 — sefer ha-torah:** "Scroll of the instruction." The LXX renders nomos consistently; EMET preserves "instruction" throughout per project conventions.

**V.10 — Hakhel:** The septennial assembly for public Torah reading. The LC marks this passage with a paragraph division (parasha setumah). The LXX Vaticanus renders the entire assembly command faithfully. EMET notes the term Hakhel is not present in the Hebrew text itself — it is a rabbinic name for the institution described in vv.10–13.

**V.14 — "commission him":** אֲצַוֶּנּוּ (atsavenu), Pi'el first-person singular. The LXX reads ἐντελοῦμαι αὐτῷ ("I will give him commands/orders"), closely parallel.

**V.16 — "go whoring":** וְזָנָה (ve-zanah), here applied to covenant infidelity. A common Hebrew idiom for apostasy. The LXX uses ἐκπορνεύσει, the same metaphor.

**V.19 — "this song":** The shirah announced here is the Ha'azinu poem of Chapter 32. The LXX pa_0163 begins the close of ch.31 and marks the transition with a chapter marker (ΛΑ = 31 in numeral).

**V.21 — yitser:** יִצְרוֹ ("his inclination/formed-impulse"), the only occurrence of this word in Deuteronomy. The LXX renders διάνοια ("thought/mind"), smoothing the anthropological force of the Hebrew. EMET renders it "inclination" to preserve its resonance with the yetser ha-ra concept in rabbinic theology.

**V.26 — "at the side of the ark":** מִצַּד אֲרוֹן (mi-tsad aron). This specifies placement beside (not within) the ark — a point of later rabbinic dispute. The LXX renders ἐν πλευρᾷ ("at the side of"), confirming the LC reading.

— *EMET*
